data.title: Mortgage rates tick down after five weeks of increase
data.description:
Mortgage rates inched down after five consecutive weeks of rising, according to the latest Freddie Mac (FMCC) Primary Mortgage Survey.

30-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 6.37% as of April 9, down from 6.46% last week and 6.62% in the same period a year ago.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.74%, down from 5.77% a week ago and 5.82% a year ago.

"The decrease in rates represents a positive development for prospective homebuyers and could spark a more favorable spring homebuying season than last year," said Sam Khater, chief economist at Freddie Mac.

data.title: Brown-Forman gains on report Sazerac approached on potential deal
data.description:
Brown-Forman (BF. A) (BF. B) rose 12% after a report that Sazerac recently approached the maker of Jack Daniels about a potential acquisition, according to a WSJ report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter. 

The report comes after a Bloomberg report late last month that Pernod Ricard SA (PDRDF) was weighing a potential purchase of Brown-Forman (BF.B).  Brown-Forman (BF.A) (BF.B) confirmed the company is engaged in discussions with Pernod Ricard.

Closely held Sazerac has 550 brands including Buffalo Trace bourbon, Fireball Cinnamon Whisky and BuzzBallz ready-to-drink cocktails.

data.title: Datadog upgraded to Buy due to AI-driven growth in data volumes: Guggenheim
data.description:
Datadog (DDOG) was upgraded to Buy from Neutral by Guggenheim as the investment firm finds the company well positioned to take advantage of AI-driven growth in data volumes and IT complexity.

Guggenheim also set a $175 price target on the stock.

Shares were down 4% during Thursday trading along with the bulk of software stocks. The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) had sunk 3.4%.

"In our view, Datadog has a sophisticated backend architecture that is a deep moat against competition and potential commoditization via LLMs or OTel, uniquely strong revenue contribution from multiple products, and is leading the pack in terms of agentic observability that we believe is net expansionary to the Observability software market," said Guggenheim analysts Howard Ma and Joseph DiBartolomeo in an investor report.

Guggenheim estimates Datadog will experience 27% revenue growth with an operating margin of 24.5% in 2026. 

"We believe that the Observability market may be understated by both future workload penetration and growth, especially when considering the emergence of agentic observability," Ma noted. "We don't believe agents will replace humans but rather supplement them to analyze an otherwise insurmountable amount of data. We see agents handling initial incident responses, but agents are lightweight by design and will require extensive human oversight. The Bits AI suite is leading in its category and could serve as a new monetization lever for Datadog, perhaps via outcome-based pricing."

data.title: Eaton to build Nebraska switchgear facility as data center demand surges
data.description:
Eaton (ETN) said it will invest more than $30 million to expand its U.S. manufacturing footprint with a new facility in Nebraska, citing rising demand for electrical equipment tied to the rapid buildout of data centers.

The company plans to construct a 370,000-square-foot plant in Bellevue, near Omaha, with production expected to begin in the first half of 2027, according to an announcement on Wednesday. The site will manufacture medium-voltage switchgear, equipment used to regulate and protect electrical systems in data centers, utilities and industrial settings.

The investment comes as power infrastructure is emerging as a potential bottleneck for the expansion of artificial intelligence and cloud computing facilities. Industry estimates point to nearly 3,000 data centers either planned or under construction across the United States, increasing pressure on supply chains for critical electrical components.

Eaton (ETN) said the Nebraska facility will focus on both air-insulated and gas-insulated switchgear, with products tailored to integrate into modular power systems commonly used in large-scale data center deployments. The company expects the site to support higher production volumes and faster delivery timelines for customers building out new capacity.

The U.S. market for switchgear is projected to expand significantly over the next decade, growing from roughly $17.8 billion in 2024 to about $31.8 billion by 2034, according to industry estimates cited by the company.

The project is expected to create more than 200 jobs across engineering, manufacturing and production roles. Hiring is set to begin later this year. Eaton said employees from its existing Omaha-area operations will gradually transition to the new facility, which is located about six miles from its current site.

Nebraska officials, including Governor Jim Pillen and Bellevue Mayor Rusty Hike, said the expansion would support local employment and strengthen the region’s role in energy infrastructure manufacturing.

Eaton (ETN) has been increasing its manufacturing capacity globally, with more than $1.5 billion invested since 2023. The company said the latest expansion is part of a broader effort to meet growing demand linked to electrification and digital infrastructure.

data.title: Amazon investing $25B in Mississippi data centers
data.description:
• Amazon (AMZN) is expanding its data center footprint in Mississippi, bringing its investment in the state to $25B, including investments in carbon-free energy and grid improvements.
  • The projects will create 2,000 “high-skilled” jobs within the state’s data center operations, although the company did not specify if these are permanent positions or construction-related.
  • The expansion includes an additional $11B investment in the Madison County data center and $1B to convert a former Delphi manufacturing plant in Hinds County to a data center facility.
  • Amazon (AMZN) has also invested in five renewable energy projects that enable 616 megawatts of new carbon-free energy in Mississippi through solar and wind farms.
  • Amazon Web Services reportedly operates nearly 400 data centers in the U.S., with the majority concentrated in the eastern part of the country, with plans to continue its rapid growth to accommodate the explosive need for artificial intelligence computing.
  • Source: Amazon News

data.title: U.K. pension fund body latest dissenter to BP Chair, other board-supported resolutions
data.description:
The Local Authority Pension Fund Forum, a group representing U.K. pension funds, said Thursday it has joined other shareholders in saying it will vote against Chair Albert Manifold's re-election and other board-supported resolutions at the company's annual general meeting later this month.

LAPFF, whose members taken together would be equivalent to a top-5 shareholder according to LSEG data, said its recommendation was the result of concerns about "transparency and robust governance."

A shareholder resolution calls on BP (BP) to show that higher spending on fossil fuel production can deliver value for shareholders and was filed earlier this year by U.K. and European pension funds along with activist investor Australasian Centre for Corporate Responsibility.

Earlier this week, shareholder Legal & General said it would vote for the resolution, and proxy advisor Glass Lewis stated its support, saying it "could provide decision-useful information for shareholders."

Manifold said the board recommended shareholders vote against the resolution, saying the extra disclosure would duplicate existing reporting by the company.

"It would pull the company in the opposite direction to where we want and need to go, which is towards simpler, standardized and comparable reporting," the chair said. "It also cuts across the board's responsibility to decide how to address disclosure for all shareholders."

data.title: Textron Aviation to showcase Citation jets, broader lineup at German air show
data.description:
Textron Aviation on Thursday announced plans to present a range of business and general aviation aircraft at AERO Friedrichshafen later this month, including the event debut of two Citation jets.

The Cessna Citation Longitude and Cessna Citation Ascend are scheduled to appear at the April 22–25 show in Friedrichshafen, Germany. The Ascend’s appearance will mark its first showing at a European industry event.

 

 

The company, a subsidiary of Textron Inc. (TXT), is expected to display the aircraft alongside models from its Cessna, Beechcraft and Pipistrel brands. The exhibition will take place at AERO Friedrichshafen, one of the region’s larger gatherings for general aviation manufacturers and operators.

In addition to the two Citation jets, Textron Aviation said it intends to bring several other aircraft to its static display, including the Cessna Citation M2 Gen2, Beechcraft King Air 260, Cessna Grand Caravan EX and Cessna Turbo Stationair HD, along with the Pipistrel Virus SW.

Additional aircraft, including the Cessna Turbo Skylane, Cessna Skyhawk, Pipistrel Velis Electro and Pipistrel Explorer, are expected to be featured at the company’s indoor exhibit space.

The company said it maintains a sizable installed base in Europe, including hundreds of business jets and turboprop aircraft operating across the region. It also operates multiple service centers and parts distribution capabilities in Europe to support aircraft operators, alongside mobile service teams and around-the-clock maintenance assistance.

Company representatives from parts, service and propeller divisions are expected to attend the event and meet with customers and industry participants during the show.

data.title: Iran attack lull tests whether Hormuz can reopen
data.description:
A sudden drop in Iranian attacks has sparked cautious optimism about de-escalation in the Middle East, though commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz remains severely disrupted.

Jim Bianco of Bianco Research flagged the dramatic shift in a social media post Thursday, noting that Iran had fired “just 7 drones today, all at Bahrain,” one day after launching 149 missiles and drones—a monthly high. 

The key question now, Bianco added, is if the lull will last and if it will be enough to “get ships moving.”

On that front, the picture remains bleak. Ship traffic through the strait is reportedly still well below 10% of normal levels despite the fragile ceasefire agreement, with only seven vessels passing in the past day compared to roughly 140 under typical conditions.

The backlog is expected to take weeks to clear, and Iran continues to tightly control how ships transit the waterway.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: PLTR, RIVN, GILD, INTC
data.description:
Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and Rivian Automotive (RIVN) have both received upgrades, with Seeking Alpha analysts pointing to Palantir’s strengthening position as NATO’s modernization engine and Rivian’s transformation into a technology-platform play backed by major partnerships with Volkswagen and Uber.

On the downgrade side, Intel (INTC) has been moved to Neutral by analyst Uttam Dey, who believes the stock’s rally following its Tesla Terafab deal has priced in too much future growth. Gilead Sciences (GILD) also receives a Hold rating as recent acquisitions, while strategically sound, will take years to meaningfully contribute to revenue.

Upgrades

  • Palantir Technologies (PLTR): Upgrade Hold to Buy by Oliver Rodzianko. The analyst cites Palantir’s role as a core modernization engine for NATO and its deepening alliance architecture, with $4.4 billion in government deal value reinforcing its competitive moat.

    “The alliance angle is one that is strong for Palantir and also potentially undertapped. NATO itself has warned that weak modernization raises interoperability gaps, and Palantir also reported about $4.4 billion of government remaining deal value from the United States and allied countries, so part of the moat sits in coalition architecture.”

  • Rivian Automotive (RIVN): Upgrade to Buy by Ragmar Rikberg. The analyst highlights Rivian’s emergence as a compelling technology-platform play beyond traditional EV manufacturing, supported by an additional $2B in funding from Volkswagen and a potential $1.25B deal with Uber for up to 50,000 AV-ready vehicles.

    “The latest announcement about the Rivian–Uber collaboration adds, in my view, yet another strong pillar supporting Rivian’s bullish narrative...The $1.25 billion total package includes an initial $300 million equity investment, expected to follow shortly after signing, along with an agreement to purchase up to 50,000 AV‑ready vehicles if Rivian meets specific autonomous‑driving milestones by set deadlines.”

Downgrades

  • Intel (INTC): Downgrade to Neutral by Uttam Dey. While the foundry partnership with Tesla’s Terafab represents a long-term win, the analyst believes INTC shares have rallied too far, now trading at approximately 5x forward sales with flat near-term revenue and major Terafab contributions not expected until 2027.

    “I believe a majority of the future revenue that markets are excited about is already baked into the stock today...While Intel is leading the charge and moving ahead on its goals to deliver a sustained inflection, the resulting exuberance from the investor community is leading to Intel’s shares trading at rich valuation premiums.”

  • Gilead Sciences (GILD): Hold by Daniel Schönberger. The analyst maintains a cautious stance as recent acquisitions of Tubulis, Ouro Medicines, and Arcellx are strategically aligned but will take years to impact revenue, with the stock appearing fairly valued based on DCF analysis.

    “In my opinion, Gilead Sciences is a "Hold" at this point, and the recent acquisitions don't change the picture much. It is rather difficult to assess whether the acquisitions were good strategic decisions and will lead to higher growth rates.”

data.title: Lynas, Glencore, Rio Tinto top large-cap Materials picks by SA Quant ahead of earnings
data.description:
As interest in critical materials rises, Lynas Rare Earths Limited of Australia ranks highest among large-cap materials stocks. Its stock (LYSCF) has gained about 82% year to date, alongside other top-ranked mining giants Glencore (GLCNF) and Rio Tinto (RIO).

Therefore, as earnings season nears, investors are turning to updated quant ratings to gauge how companies stack up on valuation, growth, profitability, momentum, and estimate revisions after recent results.

State Street Materials Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLB), meanwhile, has climbed about 14% year to date, leading sector gains even as broader markets remain volatile.

Top-quant rated stocks:

  • Lynas Rare Earths Limited (LYSDY), Quant Rating: 4.96, Strong Buy.
  • Glencore plc (GLNCY), Quant Rating: 4.95, Strong Buy.
  • Rio Tinto Group (RIO), Quant Rating: 4.93, Strong Buy.
  • Lundin Mining Corporation (LUNMF), Quant Rating: 4.93, Strong Buy.
  • Coeur Mining, Inc. (CDE), Quant Rating: 4.91, Strong Buy.

Lowest quant-rated stocks:

  • DSM-Firmenich AG (DSFIY), Quant Rating: 1.85, Sell.
  • Symrise AG (SYIEY), Quant Rating: 2.05, Sell.
  • Givaudan SA (GVDNY), Quant Rating: 2.07, Sell.
  • Heidelberg Materials AG (HDLMY), Quant Rating: 2.09, Sell.
  • Akzo Nobel N.V. (AKZOY), Quant Rating: 2.10, Sell.

Materials ETFs: (XLB), (VAW), (IYM), (FXZ), (MXI), (RSPM)
